description: Sit tall, dowel across shoulders, rotate trunk each direction; minimum standard is 45 degrees per side. Pitchers with less than 45deg to the throwing side compensate with lumbar rotation or scap protraction.
notes: Assess T-spine rotation before prescribing. relevance: a sub-45deg deficit forces the trunk to fly open instead of separating, so this is the gate test for Landon's staying-closed / separation prerequisite.

description: Sit at edge of table, hips/knees at 90, internally rotate the femur without letting the hip hike. Compares lead-leg HIR to back-leg HIR; a lead-leg deficit is common in pitchers with elbow/shoulder pain.
notes: Identifies hip IR deficit (HIRD). relevance: lead-leg HIR deficit makes the front hip open early and the lead leg land open — the root of Landon's lost separation window.

description: Cressey's catalog of the key pitcher deficits: lead-leg hip internal rotation deficit, hip extension deficit, GIRD, and T-spine restriction. Frames which limitations force mechanical compensations.
notes: When front hip opens too soon from muscular restriction, the arm lags out of the scapular plane. relevance: maps Landon's whole fault chain (early front side, push arm) to specific physical deficits to screen.

description: Side-lying with knees tucked, reach top arm open and rotate the T-spine while the floor limits ROM; drives scap retraction/depression and humeral external rotation. Entry-level T-spine rotation drill.
notes: T-spine extension/rotation should drive scap retraction, not lumbar rotation. relevance: builds the T-spine rotation capacity that lets Landon's trunk stay neutral/closed while the hips rotate (separation prerequisite).

description: Quadruped or bent-over, hand behind head, rotate the upper back open and closed isolating thoracic rotation while bracing the core so the lumbar spine stays quiet.
notes: Keep ribs down so motion comes from T-spine not low back. relevance: trains thoracic rotation independent of the pelvis — the exact capacity Landon needs to separate trunk from hips.

description: Cressey's off-season T-spine progression for throwers: side-lying windmill, bent-over rotation, foam-roller extension. T-spine delivers the scapula; a deficit lets the humeral head migrate forward and stress the pec.
notes: Inadequate T-spine mobility = forward humeral migration + pec strain. relevance: the T-spine library that underpins Landon's staying-closed and clean arm-path prerequisites.

drill_or_video_name: How Rib Cage Positioning Impacts the Pitching Delivery (article)
description: Explains that an extended posture with poor rib-cage/pelvic control makes it hard to rotate efficiently at front-foot-strike while holding the line to home. Poor control = misses up and flat breaking balls.
notes: Thorax is the center of force transfer; uncontrolled rib cage prevents efficient rotation at foot strike. relevance: directly explains Landon's shoulders-fly-open / trunk-not-neutral fault as a rib-cage-control problem.

description: Supine or half-kneeling: bring ribs down and pelvis into slight posterior tilt to re-establish the canister, then full exhalation to feel anterior core engage. Foundational for rib-cage control.
notes: Step 1 = ribs down + posterior tilt; step 2 = breathe and fully exhale in that zone. relevance: gives Landon the neutral-trunk position he must hold while the hips rotate (separation prerequisite).

description: Posterior pelvic tilt curling the pelvis toward the ribs without spinal hyperextension; teaches active control of the ribs-to-pelvis relationship under a little load.
notes: Bring pelvis and rib cage together; controls anterior pelvic tilt. relevance: anti-extension core strength so Landon doesn't dump into extension and fly open at landing.

drill_or_video_name: Pitching Performance: Trunk Position at Foot Strike (article)
description: Cressey on what good trunk position at front-foot-strike looks like and the strength/control that produces it — stacked rib cage over pelvis, controlled lateral and rotational lean.
notes: Trunk must arrive stacked and controlled at foot strike. relevance: the strength-coach picture of the braced, neutral trunk Landon currently loses when his shoulders fly open.

description: Supine, soles together; actively internally rotate the femurs so the stretch occurs at the hips not the knees. Direct hip IR mobilization. Coach the IR action to avoid knee valgus stress.
notes: Actively IR the femurs — stretch is at the hip, never let the knees collapse. relevance: opens lead-leg hip internal rotation so the front hip can post instead of swinging open early.

drill_or_video_name: Supine Dynamic Hip Internal Rotation Stretch
description: Supine dynamic drill rotating the femur into internal rotation through repeated reps to restore hip IR ROM; Cressey's pair to the knee-to-knee for HIRD.
notes: Dynamic, not held — grooves usable hip IR range. relevance: lead-leg hip IR is the prerequisite for the lead-leg block and for stopping the early front-side open.

description: Half-kneeling drill that tactfully works hip internal rotation by rotating over a fixed femur without cranking the joint. One of Cressey's favorite hip-IR drills.
notes: Work hip IR without cranking the joint. relevance: low-stress hip-IR builder for the lead-leg-block / front-hip-post prerequisite.

description: Half-kneeling adductor rock-back that mobilizes the hip while the athlete maintains core stability and neutral spine; parallels riding the back hip down the mound.
notes: Mobility WITH core stability, not just passive range. relevance: trains hip mobility under control so the back hip can clear and the front hip post without the trunk dumping open.

description: Single-leg-stance toe touch: get into one hip by activating that hip's adductors to pull back to neutral; teaches hip hinge + posterior weight shift on one leg.
notes: Get INTO the hip, then pull back to neutral with the adductors. relevance: trains single-leg hip loading/control that underpins back-hip drive and the lead-leg post.

description: Standing dips that take the hip through angles matching the pitching delivery; warm-up and off-season hip mobility for throwers.
notes: Parallels the hip angles of the delivery. relevance: builds usable hip ROM in pitching-specific positions for back-hip clearing.

description: Half-kneeling hip-flexor/anterior-hip stretch against the wall to restore hip extension while keeping ribs down and core braced (no lumbar arch).
notes: Get hip extension without arching the low back. relevance: hip-extension deficit forces a lumbar-arch compensation that throws Landon's trunk into extension and open.

drill_or_video_name: Lateral Lunge with Band Overhead Reach
description: Combo drill: lateral lunge loading the adductors/back hip while a banded overhead reach challenges rotary + anterior core — mimics riding the back hip down the mound under control.
notes: Ride the back hip down while controlling the trunk. relevance: pairs back-hip loading with anti-extension core — exactly the back-hip-drive-without-leaking pattern Landon needs.

description: Glute bridge to terminal hip extension while reaching the arms back to add thoracic rotation/mobility — couples hip extension with T-spine motion as happens in delivery.
notes: Hips extend AND T-spine keeps rotating. relevance: links posterior-chain hip extension with T-spine rotation — the back-hip-drive-into-separation coupling.

description: Single-leg hinge with a rotational reach across the planted leg; trains glute med/posterior-chain control of the pelvis on one leg under a frontal/rotational demand.
notes: Control the pelvis on one leg — don't let the knee cave. relevance: builds the single-leg glute-med strength that braces the lead leg block and stabilizes back-hip drive.

drill_or_video_name: How Strength and Mobility Impact the Pitching Stride (article)
description: Cressey on the posterior chain as a counterbalance: engage glutes/hamstrings instead of swinging the front leg into landing — start like a one-leg squat to feel glute/ham, then lateral lunge to keep adductors controlling the pelvis.
notes: Lead leg as counter-balance, not the primary power source. relevance: the strength-coach blueprint for the lead-leg block — stop swinging the front leg open, use the posterior chain to control the stride.

drill_or_video_name: Pitching Mechanics: Is Lead Leg Blocking Enough? (article)
description: Cressey argues a good lead-leg block still fails to transfer force if limited upstream — chiefly inadequate thoracic flexion and scapular upward rotation. Connects the block to the chain above it.
notes: The block alone isn't enough without T-spine + scap upward rotation upstream. relevance: tells Landon WHY a braced front side won't whip the arm unless his T-spine/scap prerequisites are also fixed.

description: Split-stance glute-med activation lifting the leg at end range to strengthen the hip abductors/external rotators that keep the knee from caving in single-leg.
notes: Glute med weakness = knee collapses medially in single-leg. relevance: strengthens the abductors that keep the lead leg from collapsing/landing open in the block.

description: Forearms on wall shoulder-width, elbows 90deg, slide up and out without shrugging; band cues serratus/lower-trap to drive scapular upward rotation so the upper trap doesn't dominate.
notes: Slide up and out, no shrug — serratus + lower trap do the rotating. relevance: scap upward-rotation deficit keeps the hand from getting out front; this cleans the arm path off a braced front side.

description: Progression of wall-slide variants (forearm, J-band, med-ball) building dynamic scapular upward rotation and serratus control for overhead positions.
notes: Serratus is the rotation component of scapular upward rotation. relevance: pitchers need more scap upward rotation than any other athlete — this is the arm-path prerequisite for whip not push.

description: Stand back to wall, ribs down, raise arms overhead keeping low back flat and ribs down; exposes whether shoulder flexion comes from the scap/T-spine or a lumbar-arch compensation.
notes: Reach overhead without arching the back or flaring the ribs. relevance: screens/trains overhead capacity so the arm can lay back off a stacked trunk instead of an extended one.

description: Prone on incline, raise arm to a Y to strengthen lower trap and correct an anteriorly-tilted scapula; troubleshooting cues keep the upper trap quiet.
notes: Lower trap drives it; keep the upper trap from taking over. relevance: corrects anterior scap tilt so the scapula upwardly rotates and posts the arm — clean arm path prerequisite.

description: Prone posterior-cuff drill: externally rotate the humeral head without anterior migration; gravity pulls the scap into anterior tilt so the lower trap must fire to counteract.
notes: ER the humeral head without it sliding forward; lower trap holds the scap. relevance: posterior-cuff + scap control that protects the shoulder and supports layback in the arm path.

description: Cressey video on dynamic scapular control for the overhead athlete — coordinating cuff and scap stabilizers through range rather than static holds.
notes: Train cuff and scap stabilizers together, dynamically. relevance: dynamic scap control is what lets the arm spiral cleanly instead of the shoulder flying open and pushing.

drill_or_video_name: Half-Kneeling Cable External Rotation - Scapular Plane
description: Half-kneeling cable ER in the scapular plane; the half-kneel locks the lower body so the cuff/scap work in a pitching-relevant plane with anti-rotation core demand.
notes: Work the cuff in the scapular plane, core braced. relevance: scap-plane cuff strength + anti-rotation core = the stable shoulder/trunk that lets the arm whip not push.

drill_or_video_name: One-Arm Band Rotational Row from Low Setting
description: Low-to-high rotational row emphasizing getting the hand up-and-out in front; Cressey programs it as the last drill before a pitcher picks up the ball.
notes: Get up and out in front. relevance: grooves the up-and-out-front scap/arm action — the pre-throw primer for getting the hand out front instead of pushing.

drill_or_video_name: 13 Tips for Mighty Elbows and Wrists (article)
description: Cressey's elbow/wrist durability tips: wrist flexion/extension, pronation/supination, radial/ulnar deviation, grip, and soft-tissue work on the flexor mass; balance the forearm musculature.
notes: Train all forearm actions + soft tissue the flexors. relevance: directly addresses Landon's flexor-pronator / medial-elbow durability — the conservative-load non-negotiable.

description: Elastic-band resisted forearm pronation; research-validated to selectively activate the flexor-pronator mass that dynamically stabilizes the elbow against valgus.
notes: Pronation under band load lights up the flexor-pronator mass. relevance: builds the dynamic valgus stabilizer protecting Landon's medial elbow — his primary injury risk.

description: Resisted ulnar deviation targeting flexor carpi ulnaris, the biggest dynamic protector of the UCL during throwing.
notes: FCU is the #1 dynamic protector of the UCL. relevance: strengthens the exact muscle that mitigates medial-elbow gapping for Landon's flexor-pronator history.

drill_or_video_name: Palmar Fascia / Forearm Flexor Soft-Tissue Work
description: Self soft-tissue work on the palmar fascia and anteromedial forearm flexors to manage tissue quality; pair with Graston/ART-style work for symptomatic elbows.
notes: Keep the flexor mass supple — forearm tightness is the warning sign. relevance: tissue-quality maintenance on the flexor-pronator mass; forearm tightness is Landon's documented early warning.

description: Cressey on the common flexor tendon anatomy (FCR, FCU, palmaris, FDS, pronator teres) and how the flexor-pronator mass dynamically stabilizes the elbow against valgus load.
notes: The flexor-pronator mass is the dynamic valgus stabilizer of the elbow. relevance: explains WHY pronator/flexor strengthening protects Landon's medial elbow — the rationale for the durability block.
